page.title: Marbella Full-Day Island Hopping Boat Tour: Explore Coastal Gems

data.meta_description: Discover the best island hopping boat tour Marbella offers. Visit Cala del Faro & Cala Cortés on a full-day charter from Puerto Banús. Book now!
data.summary: This full-day island hopping boat tour Marbella takes you to hidden coves like Cala del Faro and Cala Cortés, with stops for swimming, snorkelling, and lunch. Departing from Puerto Banús, our fleet includes luxury yachts with skipper, drinks, and snacks included.
key_facts: Full-day (8-hour) island hopping tour from Puerto Banús
Visit Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, and other coastal gems
Licensed skipper, fuel, insurance, and Spanish IVA included
Complimentary drinks (water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava) and light snacks
Maximum 12 passengers on most yachts; smaller boats for intimate groups
Swimming, snorkelling, and sunbathing stops at secluded coves
Flexible itinerary; lunch stop at a beach club or on board

faq.q: What is included in the island hopping boat tour Marbella?
faq.a: Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). Lunch is not included but can be arranged at a beach club or ordered as a catering upgrade.

faq.q: Which coves do we visit on the tour?
faq.a: Typical stops include Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, and the coastline near Cabopino. The skipper will tailor the route based on sea conditions and your preferences. You can also request a longer run to Estepona or Sotogrande.

faq.q: How many people can join the tour?
faq.a: Our fleet includes boats for 5 to 12 passengers. For example, the Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 carry up to 9–11 guests, while the Mangusta 80 and Ferretti 94 take up to 12. Smaller groups can book the Mariah SX21 (5 pax) or the Speedboat (6 pax).

faq.q: Can we bring our own food and drinks?
faq.a: Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and additional drinks. Our standard package already includes a selection of beverages and snacks. If you prefer a catered lunch, we can arrange that for an extra fee.

faq.q: What time does the full-day tour start and end?
faq.a: Full-day charters typically run from 10:00 to 18:00 or 11:00 to 19:00. Departure is from Puerto Banús. We recommend confirming the exact timing when booking, as it can be adjusted to your needs.

faq.q: Do I need a licence to drive the boat?
faq.a: No. All our charters come with a licensed skipper who handles navigation and safety. You can simply relax and enjoy the ride.

faq.q: What happens if the weather is bad?
faq.a: Safety is our priority. If the forecast shows high winds or rough seas, we will reschedule your tour or offer a full refund. The skipper will also adjust the route to sheltered areas if conditions change.

faq.q: How do I book the island hopping boat tour Marbella?
faq.a: You can book directly through our website or contact us via WhatsApp for availability and pricing on specific yachts. For boats not listed with fixed rates, we provide a custom quote within minutes.

data.body_html:
Escape the Costa del Sol crowds and spend a full day exploring the Mediterranean's hidden corners on an island hopping boat tour Marbella. Departing from Puerto Banús, you'll cruise along the coast to secluded coves, swim in crystal-clear waters, and lunch at a beach club or on board. All charters include a licensed skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, and insurance — so you just relax.

Why Choose a Full-Day Island Hopping Boat Tour Marbella?

A full-day charter gives you time to visit multiple spots without rushing. Unlike a half-day trip, you can sail further south to Estepona or east to Cala del Faro, drop anchor for a long swim, enjoy a leisurely lunch, and still have time for sunbathing. Our fleet includes yachts from 6.4m to 29m, so whether you're a couple or a group of 12, there's a boat for you.

Typical Itinerary for a Full-Day Island Hopping Tour

Your skipper will tailor the route based on sea conditions and your preferences, but here's a common plan:

  • 10:00 – Departure from Puerto Banús. Welcome drink and safety briefing.
  • 10:30 – Cruise east towards Cabopino. Pass the Marbella Marina and Golden Mile.
  • 11:15 – First stop at Cala del Faro for swimming and snorkelling. Calm, clear water with rocky cliffs.
  • 12:30 – Sail to Cala Cortés, a quieter cove with sandy patches. Anchor for a longer swim.
  • 13:30 – Lunch stop. Either at a beach club (e.g., Nikki Beach or Ocean Club) or a picnic on board.
  • 15:00 – Head west towards Estepona or stay near Marbella for a final dip.
  • 17:00 – Return cruise to Puerto Banús with drinks and snacks.
  • 18:00 – Disembark.

Our Fleet for Island Hopping

Choose from these popular yachts for your island hopping boat tour Marbella:

BoatLengthMax PaxPrice (8h)
Astondoa 4012.5m9€2,299
Azimut 3912.5m11€2,299
Mangusta 8024m12from €4,719 (4h min)
Fairline Targa 12m12m10On request
Pershing 4614m10On request

All prices include skipper, fuel, drinks, snacks, insurance, and VAT. For boats with 'On request' pricing, contact us via WhatsApp for a quote.

What's Included in Your Charter

Every island hopping boat tour Marbella comes with:

  • Licensed, English-speaking skipper
  • Fuel for the full day
  • Water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, and cava
  • Light snacks (crisps, nuts, olives)
  • Insurance and safety equipment
  • Spanish IVA (21%)

Lunch is not included but can be arranged at a beach club or as a catering upgrade. You're also welcome to bring your own food.

Best Stops for Swimming and Snorkelling

The coast between Marbella and Estepona is dotted with coves. Our top picks:

  • Cala del Faro – Rocky cove with a lighthouse. Clear water, good snorkelling. Sheltered from westerly winds.
  • Cala Cortés – Small sandy beach backed by cliffs. Quiet, with excellent visibility.
  • Cabo Pino – Sandy beach and dunes. Shallow water, ideal for families.
  • Estepona coast – Several unnamed coves near the old town. Deeper water for jumping in.

How to Book Your Island Hopping Boat Tour Marbella

Booking is simple. Browse our full fleet and choose your preferred yacht. For fixed-price boats like the Astondoa 40 or Azimut 39, you can book directly online. For others, send us a WhatsApp message with your date, group size, and preferred boat — we'll reply with availability and a quote within minutes.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is included in the island hopping boat tour Marbella?

Every charter includes a licensed skipper, fuel, water, soft drinks, beer, white wine, cava, light snacks, insurance, safety equipment, and Spanish IVA (21%). Lunch is not included but can be arranged at a beach club or ordered as a catering upgrade.

Which coves do we visit on the tour?

Typical stops include Cala del Faro, Cala Cortés, and the coastline near Cabopino. The skipper will tailor the route based on sea conditions and your preferences. You can also request a longer run to Estepona or Sotogrande.

How many people can join the tour?

Our fleet includes boats for 5 to 12 passengers. For example, the Astondoa 40 and Azimut 39 carry up to 9–11 guests, while the Mangusta 80 and Ferretti 94 take up to 12. Smaller groups can book the Mariah SX21 (5 pax) or the Speedboat (6 pax).

Can we bring our own food and drinks?

Yes, you are welcome to bring your own food and additional drinks. Our standard package already includes a selection of beverages and snacks. If you prefer a catered lunch, we can arrange that for an extra fee.

What time does the full-day tour start and end?

Full-day charters typically run from 10:00 to 18:00 or 11:00 to 19:00. Departure is from Puerto Banús. We recommend confirming the exact timing when booking, as it can be adjusted to your needs.

Do I need a licence to drive the boat?

No. All our charters come with a licensed skipper who handles navigation and safety. You can simply relax and enjoy the ride.

What happens if the weather is bad?

Safety is our priority. If the forecast shows high winds or rough seas, we will reschedule your tour or offer a full refund. The skipper will also adjust the route to sheltered areas if conditions change.

How do I book the island hopping boat tour Marbella?

You can book directly through our website or contact us via WhatsApp for availability and pricing on specific yachts. For boats not listed with fixed rates, we provide a custom quote within minutes.
